page.title: endometriosis — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for endometriosis: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for endometriosis: 116 alterations and 477 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Acupuncture for clinical improvement of endometriosis-related pain.
Antioxidant vitamins supplementation reduce endometriosis related pelvic pain.
Dietary antioxidant supplementation seems to beneficially impact the severity of endometriosis-related dysmenorrhea.
Vitamin C and E antioxidant supplementation may significantly reduce pain symptoms in endometriosis.
Exposure to phthalates is associated with the occurrence of endometriosis and affects women's reproductive prognosis and ovarian function.
A higher intake of fruits is associated with a lower risk of endometriosis.
Acupuncture appears effective in treating dysmenorrhea associated with endometriosis.
Acupuncture is an effective and safe method of relieving dysmenorrhea, shortening the pain duration, and improving wellbeing and quality of life in women with endometriosis.
Acupuncture treatment on specific acupuncture points appears to be an effective pain treatment for endometriosis.
An association exists between increased PCB and p,p'-DDE serum concentrations and the risk of endometriosis.
Antioxidant supplementation reduces endometriosis-related pelvic pain in humans.
Association between vitamin D and endometriosis among American women.
Astaxanthin ameliorates inflammation, oxidative stress, and reproductive outcomes in endometriosis patients.
Causal effects of gut microbiome on endometriosis.
Chinese herbal medicine has therapeutic value in the treatment of endometriosis.
Deficient intake of antioxidants is associated with an intensification of endometriosis severity.
Ear-electroacupuncture has therapeutic value in the treatment of endometriosis-induced dysmenorrhea in patients
Emodin inhibits the migration and invasion abilities of human endometrial stromal cells.
Exposure to bisphenols may increase the risk of endometriosis
Exposure to high levels of PFAS in drinking water was associated with increased risk of PCOS and possibly uterine leiomyoma.
Higher omega-3 fatty acid consumption is associated with a reduced risk for endometriosis.
Japanese-style acupuncture may be an effective, safe, and well-tolerated adjunct therapy for endometriosis-related pelvic pain in adolescents.
Low levels of progesterone in the follicular phase could be responsible for the progression of endometriosis and related pain.
Mediterranean diet and oxidative stress: A relationship with pain perception in endometriosis.
Nanomicelle curcumin improves oxidative stress, inflammatory markers, and assisted reproductive techniques outcomes in endometriosis cases.
Oral NAC improves endometriosis-related pain and the size of endometriomas.
Potentized estrogen at a dose of 3 drops twice daily for 24 weeks was effective in reducing endometriosis-associated pelvic pain.
Resveratrol lowers VEGF and TNF-α 2 expression in endometriosis women.
Silymarin significantly reduced interleukin-6 levels, sizes of endometrioma lesions, and pain-related symptoms.
Supplementation with vitamin D or ω-3 fatty acids in adolescent girls and young women with endometriosis.
The modulating effects of vitamin D on the activity of β-catenin in the endometrium of women with endometriosis.
The present findings support the potential role of antioxidants in the management of endometriosis.
There are increased levels of polychlorobiphenyls in Italian women with endometriosis.
These findings showed that people with Vitamin D deficiency are at higher risk of endometriosis.
These results highlight the potential association between BPA exposure and endometriosis.
Vaginal parturition decreases recurrence of endometriosis.
Vitamin D intake in patients with endometriosis resulted in a significant improvement of pelvic pain, total-/HDL-cholesterol ratio, hs-CRP and TAC levels.
Vitamins C and E supplementation was associated with a decrease in the concentration of oxidative stress markers in women with endometriosis.
Women with endometriosis improved their peripheral antioxidant markers after the application of a high antioxidant diet.
Yoga practice was associated with a reduction in levels of chronic pelvic pain and an improvement in QoL in women with endometriosis.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Endometriosis lesions may partially regress after menopause; spontaneous resolution of symptoms rare; recurrence rate after surgery 20-40% at 2 years and 50-60% at 5 years
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Excision surgery achieves pain relief in 60-80% at 6 months but 50% recurrence at 5 years; GnRH agonists + add-back therapy: 70-90% pain reduction but oestrogen-deprivation side effects; combined OCP: 50-60% pain reduction; elagolix (Orilissa): 43% reduction in dysmenorrhoea vs placebo (ELARIS EM-I)
remission.gap_size: Very large — diagnostic delay averages 7-10 years from symptom onset; no disease-modifying treatment; high recurrence after surgery
remission.barrier_detail: Diagnostic delay of 7-10 years is the largest modifiable factor; endometriosis dismissed as 'normal period pain'; no non-surgical diagnostic test; all medical treatments are suppressive not curative; recurrence after surgery is universal without continued medical suppression
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — linzagolix (Yselty, GnRH antagonist) approved in Europe; 2021 relugolix combination (Myfembree) FDA approved; elagolix (Orilissa) 2018; GnRH agonists standard since 1980s; excision surgery approach unchanged for decades
remission.outdated_treatments: Danazol (androgenic; significant side effects including virilisation, liver toxicity; replaced by GnRH analogues and GnRH antagonists); medroxyprogesterone acetate injections as long-term therapy without bone density monitoring; diagnostic laparoscopy without concurrent excision (two-step procedure causing additional delay to treatment)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Dietary anti-inflammatory approaches (omega-3, low-inflammatory diet — observational data on symptom reduction); N-acetylcysteine (NAC) — Italian RCT showing endometrioma regression; melatonin (anti-inflammatory, analgesic — small RCTs positive); cannabidiol (CBD) for pelvic pain — Phase 2 underway
remission.frontier_signal: Dichloroacetate (DCA) — metabolic reprogramming of endometriosis cells (Phase 2); progesterone receptor modulators (mifepristone low-dose; ulipristal — EU regulatory issues after liver toxicity); stem cell-based endometrial repair; JAK inhibitors for ectopic lesion suppression
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
